bio.education: Moorpark High School California University of Pennsylvania
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Son of Dave and Elaine Harris
  • Has a brother Sean
  • Began playing field hockey in 1990
  • Game Day Mantra: "Routine, focus on the next moment, have fun."
  • Favorite Sports Quote: "First I went left, he did too. Then I went right, and he did too. Then I went left again, and he went to buy a hot dog." - Zlatan Ibrahimovic
  • Best Words of Advice: “There is a fine line between passion and obsession. Be passionate about what you do in life, but don’t obsess so much that you forget to enjoy what you are doing.”
Career Highlights
  • Indoor World Cup, Pretoria, 2023 (Team - Men)
  • Pan American Games, Santiago, 2023 (Team - Men)
  • Pan American Games, Lima, 2019 (Team - Men)
  • Pan American Games, Toronto, 2015 (Team - Men)
  • Indoor World Cup, Poznan, 2011 (Team - Men)
World Championship Experience
  • Gold - 2023 Rohrmax Cup (Indoor) (Vienna, Austria)
  • Gold - 2012 World League Round 1 (Chula Vista, Calif.), Top Goal Scorer of the Tournament Award
  • Gold - 2008 Indoor Pan American Cup (San Juan, Argentina), Top Scorer of the Tournament Award
  • Silver - 2021 Indoor Pan American Cup (Spring City, Pa.), Top Scorer of the Tournament Award
  • Silver - 2019 3 Nations Invitational (Benalmadena, Spain), Player of the Tournament Award
  • Silver - 2010 Indoor Pan American Cup (Barquisimeto, Venezuela)
  • Silver - 2002 Indoor Pan American Cup (Rockville, Md.)
  • Bronze - 2019 Pan American Games (Lima, Peru)
  • Bronze - 2017 Pan American Cup (Lancaster, Pa.)
  • Bronze - 2017 World League Round 2 (Tacarigua, Trinidad & Tobago)
  • Silver - 2005 Indoor Pan American Cup (Kitchener, Canada)
  • Silver - 2009 Pan American Cup (Santiago, Chile)
  • 4th - 2023 FIH Indoor Hockey World Cup (Pretoria, South Africa)
  • 4th - 2022 Pan American Cup (Santiago, Chile)
  • 4th - 2019 Hockey Series Final (Bhubaneswar, India)
  • 5th - 2015 Pan American Games (Toronto, Canada)
  • 5th - 2013 World League Round 2 (Rio de Janeiro, Brazil)
  • 7th - 2015 World League Round 2 (Chula Vista, Calif.)
  • 7th - 2011 Champions Challenge II (Lille, France), Champions Challenge II Player of the Tournament Award
  • 11th - 2011 FIH Indoor Hockey World Cup (Poznan, Poland)
  • Canceled - 2022 FIH Indoor Hockey World Cup (Liège, Belgium)
More
Representing the red, white and blue since 2000, Harris has committed his countless minutes and hours to the U.S. Men’s National Team – a sheer passion that was instilled in him by his father. A hockey enthusiast himself, Harris’ father signed Harris up for a local field hockey league at the age of five. Never putting down the stick since, Harris went on to achieve accolades such as the MVP of the 2007 USA Field Hockey National Championship in Chula Vista, Calif. and was named the top goal scorer of the International Hockey Federation's World League Round 1 tournament in Chula Vista, Cali. Harris was also a key contributor in Team USA's second place finish at the 2009 Pan American Cup in Santiago, Chile. A natural when it comes to the sport, Harris would like to believe that he didn’t choose the sport, but rather, the sport chose him.

Quick Facts
  • Son of Gwendolyn Niebler and James Harris
  • Has an older sister, Gabriella
  • Enjoys snowboarding and basketball
  • His favorite dive is 5337D on 3-meter and his favorite diver is Xie Siyi
  • His first junior nationals was in 2014 and his first senior nationals was in 2017
  • Between dives, Nick likes to talk to people and listen to music (particularly pop, hip hop and rap)
  • Nick's typical pre-competition meal is a banana and a waffle
  • Outside of competition, Nick enjoys shrimp tacos
  • He describes himself as motivated, adventurous and enthusiastic
Career Highlights
  • World Junior Championships, Montreal, 2022 (3m Springboard - Men, 3m Springboard Synchro - Men)

Quick Facts
Marck Harrison’s began playing boccia in 2008. He has his eyes set on competing on the U.S. Team in Rio. He’s come a long way since then, as he’s currently ranked fourth in the BC2 class. He also competes with his club team, the Columbus Jack Attack, in Columbus, Ohio, where he was born and raised.
Marck says competing on the boccia national team allows him to fulfill his dreams, as well as create avenues for his independence.

  • Purchased her first horse and began to train for national and international competition
  • Born with a rare genetic disease called hereditary spastic paraplegia, a progressive impairment that causes muscle wasting and paralysis from the middle of the back down

bio.biography:
Career Highlights
  • Pan American Games, Lima, 2019 (Team - Women)
More

Individual Team Handball Career: 

2015: Women's Select "Big Four" Handball Challenge, 1st place (LATHC)

Other: 

2009-2012: Played Div. III basketball (forward) at Chapman University 

Personal:

"Liz" was in London for the 2012 Summer Olympic Games, filming a short documentary on Fauja Singh, at the time, a 101 year old marathon runner carrying the Olympic Torch in the relay. During her down time, she witnessed Michael Phelps win gold medals in swimming and Team USA win gold in gymnastics and beach volleyball. As a result, she caught the Olympic spirit.

Liz was a three-year varsity basketball player at Chapman University, and led her team in field goal percentage, and was second in field goals made and all rebounding categories in her senior year. While in high school, Liz competed in hurdles for Track and Field, and played basketball, earning first-team All-Trinity League as a senior, along with being First Team All-CIF (California Interscholastic Federation) Southern Section. She also played soccer for 10 years.

Liz completed her Master in Business Administration in June of 2019 at University of California, Irvine, and enjoys working in the family business, a women-owned trucking company in the Southern California Construction Industry.

bio.fun_fact: Took boxing lessons as a teenager to improve her quickness for hockey
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Position: Defenseman
  • Daughter of David and Martha Harvey
  • Has two siblings, Nolan and Grace
  • Took boxing lessons as a teenager to improve her quickness for hockey
Biographical Information

Caroline Harvey started to play ice hockey at age three and told her aunt at age three that she would make the 2022 U.S. Olympic Team in women’s ice hockey.

Harvey did make her Olympic debut at the Olympic Winter Games Beijing 2022 where she won a silver medal as the youngest member of the team. She was named best defender at the 2023 and 2025 IIHF Women's World Championships in helping Team USA winning gold both times. Harvey is also a two-time NCAA champion for the Wisconsin Badgers coached by Mark Johnson, a member of the “Miracle on Ice” team that won gold in Lake Placid in 1980. 

When Harvey is not on the ice, she enjoys showing off her dance moves on TikTok and those close to her, call her "KK" because her baby sister, Grace, could not pronounce Caroline when she was younger.

Olympic Experience
  • 2-time Olympian; 2-time Olympic medalist (1 gold, 1 silver)
    • Olympic Games Milan 2026, gold (Team - Women)
    • Olympic Games Beijing 2022, silver (Team - Women)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2025 – gold (Team - Women)
  • Years of participation: Team - Women 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025
  • Medals: 5 (2 gold, 3 silver)
    • Gold – 2025 (Team - Women); 2023 (Team - Women)
    • Silver – 2024 (Team - Women); 2022 (Team - Women); 2021 (Team - Women)
